summary: No-ship: teardown can permanently skip cleanup after a corrupt config abort.

findings.title: Ledger is advanced even when corrupt config aborts cleanup, so deselected coord config can remain permanently installed
findings.recommendation: Do not update `ledger[compositionId]` for deselected coord fittings whose hook or MCP cleanup aborted, or otherwise retain them for retry on the next `up`. As written, a corrupt `settings.json` or `claude.json` causes cleanup to abort safely, but the ledger is still rewritten to the current selected set, so once the file is repaired `reconcileCoordTeardown` no longer knows that fitting was previously selected and will not retry stripping its owner-tagged hooks or named MCP entries.
